justification: All three files created matching MJ's naming spec (coupon-repository.ts, coupon-service.ts, coupon-service.test.ts). CouponRepository is explicitly a pure interface. CouponService injects the repository via constructor. All 5 ACs implemented with exact error codes from Bird's spec (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, and 10% discount calculation). Tests use a lightweight makeMockRepo factory with no real database calls. files_changed lists all three files with purposes. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to explicit test names.

observations.text: CouponRepository is explicitly described as a 'pure interface', not a class — exactly what the spec requires
All 5 error codes are exact matches to Bird's spec: COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET
AC5 (single-use check) is fully covered with both positive and negative cases, avoiding the common oversight
Mock repository uses a clean factory pattern (makeMockRepo) — no real I/O in tests
AC1 tested with specific values and includes boundary cases like fractional totals ($55.50 → $49.95)
Guard order is logical and documented: not-found → expired → single-use → minimum order → calculation
Typed CouponError subclass with code property matches the spec's error object structure
Minor deviation: CouponError constructor is (message, code) instead of potentially spec'd (code, message) — documented and justified, no functional impact
Includes edge case tests beyond the 5 ACs: boundary minimum order, unused single-use success, multi-use with used flag
